concerns.text: Task T018 validates FR-008 on BEIR data ('Correlation check... on BEIR sample'), but FR-008 explicitly requires validation against 'code diversity' on a 'held-out code sample'. The task silently relaxes the domain constraint of the requirement, violating the spec's specific metric definition.

Tasks T021-T032 implement logic for 'proxy correlation' (relevance scores/text length) but fail to address the orphaned Success Criteria SC-002 (False Positive Rate for collapse) and SC-006 (accuracy of forecasting independent stability). The plan pivots away from 'collapse prediction' (FR-002/FR-004) without creating tasks to measure these specific SCs against the new proxy targets or formally dropping the SCs, leaving acceptance criteria unverified.

Task T005 specifies 'checksum verification' but omits the Constitution Principle III requirement to record these checksums specifically in 'state/projects/PROJ-976-llmxive-follow-up-extending-trust-region.yaml' under 'artifact_hashes'. This risks non-compliance with the project's versioning discipline.

Task T030 implements a permutation test (FR-006) but the spec defines FR-006 as testing correlation against 'collapse onset'. Since the plan abandons 'collapse' labels in favor of proxy targets, the task must explicitly state it is testing against the *proxy* null distribution; otherwise, it attempts to implement a requirement (collapse correlation) that the plan has silently discarded.
